summary: Frontend-only landing page tested at desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844). All 14 critical assertions passed (100%). Page loads without console errors, 3D phone canvas renders via WebGL with the app screenshot textured on display, all 5 Play Store CTAs link correctly to https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.bradwear.app with target=_blank, all required data-testid attributes (navbar, hero, about, 5 feature cards, gallery marquee with 16 screenshots, 4 download steps, 5 FAQ items, footer phone/email/website) are present, FAQ accordion expands/collapses on click, anchor links scroll to sections, and all 16 marquee screenshot images load successfully from googleusercontent. Mobile layout is responsive — hero headline visible, no horizontal overflow.

issues:
Minor: textContent reads 'Tampil Tajam.Percaya Diri.' with no space between sentences because the
separates lines visually but inline text has no whitespace — visually correct, only relevant for screen readers/SEO; consider adding a space or aria-label.

critical_code_review_comments: App.js is 749 lines — consider splitting components (Hero/Phone3D, Features, Screenshots, DownloadSteps, FAQ, Footer) into separate files under src/components/ for maintainability.
TextureLoader in HeroCanvas falls back to setTex(null) on error but never retries; if first screenshot URL ever 404s the phone screen will stay black. Acceptable for now.

data-testid='hero-3d-canvas' is on the wrapper <div>, not the actual <canvas> element (R3F renders canvas as child). Tests must drill down with .querySelector('canvas') — works but slightly misleading naming.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing page is fully functional. All testids in place. WebGL 3D phone renders correctly. If asked to retest, focus on visual regression (hero, gallery marquee animation) and any new sections. No backend/auth flows exist.
